why_them: Their current site runs on Joomla with index.php URL paths and has a broken SSL certificate — browsers display security warnings to visitors. The contact email is a Hotmail address, which undercuts professional credibility for a 150-year-old practice. There is no online booking system, no client portal, and a development test site suggests they have attempted a rebuild but never completed it.
recommended_service: New WordPress website with SSL, online appointment booking, and professional email setup
outreach_hook: Your website is currently showing SSL security warnings to every visitor — that alone is costing you new clients.

why_them: The site is built on Drupal with an early-2010s visual design — fixed-width grid, flat stock imagery and almost no mobile-first thinking. There is no online appointment booking anywhere on the site, forcing all scheduling through phone calls. The practice has two locations but the design provides no intuitive way for clients to navigate between them or register online.
recommended_service: Modern responsive website rebuild with multi-branch navigation and online booking integration
outreach_hook: Your Drupal site looks like it was built a decade ago — a modern redesign would let clients book online instead of always calling.

why_them: Built on the Yell website builder, the site contains live Lorem ipsum placeholder text in the hero slider — a significant trust issue for new clients. Despite trading since 1937, there is no online booking, no pet health plan sign-up, and the btconnect.com email address signals an outdated digital setup. Multiple conflicting Google Tag Manager implementations suggest the site has been patched repeatedly without a coherent strategy.
recommended_service: Custom WordPress website with online booking, branded email, and Google Analytics setup
outreach_hook: Your homepage still shows Lorem ipsum placeholder text in the hero — first impressions like that push potential clients straight to a competitor.

why_them: Dunmuir has three separate domain names in active use, fragmenting SEO authority and confusing clients. The newer Wix site has a contemporary look but no visible online booking or client account functionality. The older site remains indexed by Google and shows a mid-2010s design with no social media integration.
recommended_service: Domain consolidation, SEO audit, and Wix-to-WordPress migration with online booking
outreach_hook: Three different websites under your practice name are splitting your Google rankings — consolidating them could significantly boost how many local clients find you.

why_them: Built on the BaseKit/Names.co.uk platform circa 2017, the site has no online appointment booking and no email address visible on the homepage. The footer social media links point to generic Names.co.uk accounts rather than the practice's own Facebook page, meaning any social engagement is wasted. With two branch surgeries there is no modern branch-switching or service-filtering functionality.
recommended_service: WordPress website rebuild with branch-specific pages, contact forms, and online booking
outreach_hook: Your footer social links point to the website builder's own accounts rather than your practice — you're sending clients nowhere every time they try to follow you.

why_them: Dryfe Vets has one of the cleaner websites in D&G — built on GoDaddy Website Builder with modern layout and clear contact details. The main gap is no native online booking system, relying on PetsApp for client messaging rather than a true appointment scheduler. With 40-plus years in Lockerbie, there is an opportunity to build on their strong reputation with a SEO-optimised, independently hosted site.
recommended_service: SEO strategy and migration from GoDaddy to a custom WordPress build with integrated booking
outreach_hook: Your GoDaddy site looks great, but switching to an independent platform would give you far more control over SEO and client bookings.

why_them: Creebridge Veterinary Centre has no website at all — their entire digital presence consists of a Facebook page and third-party directory listings. They use a Gmail address as their professional contact, which undermines credibility with new clients. Serving both companion and farm animals in Newton Stewart with weekend opening hours, they are leaving significant new client acquisition to chance with no searchable web presence of their own.
recommended_service: New WordPress website with branded email, contact forms, service pages, and Google Business optimisation
outreach_hook: Without a website, every new pet owner searching for a vet in Newton Stewart finds your competitors first — not you.

why_them: A five-branch employee-owned practice covering Dumfries, Lockerbie, Annan, Langholm and Moffat, built on WordPress with the Divi page builder. Despite the multi-branch scale there is no online appointment booking — all scheduling requires a phone call — and the Divi-built layout feels generic rather than differentiated. A practice of this size with five locations is significantly under-investing in digital client acquisition.
recommended_service: Multi-location SEO strategy, online booking integration, and Divi-to-custom-theme redesign
outreach_hook: Running five branches without online booking means your phones are handling work a well-built website could be doing automatically.
